referrals.title: Constitutionalize Partisan Primaries
referrals.description: Would have required partisan primaries, blocking open primary reform
referrals.notes: Attempted to block Prop 140 open primary initiative

referrals.title: Geographic Signature Distribution for Initiatives
referrals.description: Would have required initiative signatures from all 30 legislative districts
referrals.notes: Would have made citizen initiatives significantly harder to qualify

referrals.title: Auto-Terminate Emergency Declarations
referrals.description: Would have automatically terminated emergency declarations after 30 days without legislative extension
referrals.notes: Response to COVID-era emergency powers debate

referrals.title: Pre-Election Challenges to Initiative Constitutionality
referrals.description: Would have allowed courts to strike down ballot initiatives before voters could vote on them
referrals.notes: Critics argued it would let courts preemptively kill voter initiatives

referrals.title: End Retention Election Term Limits for Judges
referrals.description: Would have ended term limits for state court judges, replacing retention elections with indefinite terms
referrals.notes: Failed by the largest margin of any 2024 referral

referrals.title: Lower Minimum Wage for Tipped Workers
referrals.description: Would have allowed employers to pay tipped workers 25% below minimum wage if tips make up the difference
referrals.notes: Would have undermined voter-approved minimum wage from Prop 206 (2016)

referrals.title: First Responder Death Benefit
referrals.description: Creates $250,000 death benefit paid from state fund for first responders killed in the line of duty

referrals.title: Property Tax Refund for Non-Enforcement of Nuisance Laws
referrals.description: Allows property owners to claim a property tax refund if local government fails to enforce laws against illegal camping, loitering, etc.
referrals.notes: Targeted at homelessness encampments; critics called it unfunded mandate

referrals.title: Mandatory Life Sentence for Child Sex Trafficking
referrals.description: Mandatory life sentence for anyone convicted of sex trafficking a child

referrals.title: Secure the Border Act
referrals.description: State-level border enforcement including criminalizing border crossings and allowing state/local law enforcement to arrest and deport
referrals.notes: Modeled after Texas SB4; potential federal preemption challenges

referrals.title: Legislative Approval for Costly Agency Rules
referrals.description: Would have required legislative approval of any state agency rule estimated to cost more than $500,000 over 5 years
referrals.notes: Critics argued it would slow down public health and safety regulations

referrals.title: Legislature Can Repeal Partially Unconstitutional Initiatives
referrals.description: Would have allowed the legislature to repeal entire voter-approved initiatives if a court found any part unconstitutional
referrals.notes: Seen as attack on Voter Protection Act

referrals.title: Single-Subject Requirement for Initiatives
referrals.description: Requires citizen initiatives to address only a single subject, matching the existing rule for legislation
referrals.notes: Has been used to challenge subsequent initiatives in court

referrals.title: Legislature Sets Property Tax Exemptions
referrals.description: Gives legislature authority to set and adjust property tax exemptions

referrals.title: Create Lieutenant Governor Office
referrals.description: Creates the office of Lieutenant Governor, running on a joint ticket with the Governor starting in 2026
referrals.notes: Arizona was one of the few states without a Lieutenant Governor

referrals.title: Supermajority for Tax-Increasing Ballot Measures
referrals.description: Requires 60% supermajority for any ballot measure that raises taxes
referrals.notes: Passed by slim margin; effectively killed Prop 208 (education funding surcharge) approach

referrals.title: In-State Tuition for Non-Citizen Residents
referrals.description: Allows non-citizen Arizona residents (including DACA recipients) who attended Arizona high school 2+ years to receive in-state tuition

referrals.title: Sales Tax for Fire Districts
referrals.description: Statewide 0.1% sales tax increase to fund fire districts
referrals.notes: Only statutory referral in 2022 to fail
